data.title: Pacific Sports Exchange 10-Q: Revenue $0 (Q) / $2,000 (YTD); EPS loss $(0.001) Q
data.description:
Pacific Sports Exchange reported minimal sales in its Form 10-Q, recording revenue of $0 for the three months ended February 28, 2026 and $2,000 for the six months ended February 28, 2026, with a net loss of $(11,842) for the quarter and $(21,923) year-to-date, translating to basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.001) for the quarter and $(0.00) year-to-date. The results reflect very limited revenue generation and operating losses driven primarily by professional fees and related operating expenses. Management noted organizational changes that place strategic control with a new chief executive and sole director.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 (three months ended Feb 28, 2026); $2,000 (six months ended Feb 28, 2026), reflecting minimal sales activity.
  • Gross Profit: $1,100 (three months ended Feb 28, 2026); $200 (six months ended Feb 28, 2026), a decline versus prior periods.
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations of $(11,842) (three months) and $(21,923) (six months), driven by professional fees.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(11,842) (three months) and $(21,923) (six months), an increased loss versus prior year periods.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted loss per common share of $(0.001) (three months) and $(0.00) (six months), reflecting a small share base.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue remains minimal and largely flat, with $2.2K for the six months ended Feb 28, 2026 versus $2.0K previously, indicating limited sales traction.
  • Sales Channel Focus: Operates primarily as an online-only retailer, relying on eBay as its main marketplace while working to optimize its company website.
  • Product & Sourcing Strategy: Resells new and used tennis and golf equipment and golf carts sourced from retailers in Florida and California, trade shows and consignment arrangements.
  • Operational Shift: Expanded into consignment sales and increased inventory of used golf carts to diversify product mix and margin opportunities.
  • Organizational Change: A change in control resulted in appointment of a new CEO and sole director, enabling unilateral strategic direction and execution.

Original SEC Filing: Pacific Sports Exchange Inc. [ PSPX ] - 10-Q - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Prudential Financial Stock Is Falling on Report of Potential Misconduct at Japan Insurer — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Janet H. Cho

Prudential Financial Inc. stock is falling on Tuesday after a report about potential employee misconduct at Japan-based Gibraltar Life Insurance.

Japan's Nikkei.com news site reported Tuesday that "dozens of cases of suspected financial fraud by former employees of Gibraltar Life Insurance, a subsidiary of the Prudential Group have surfaced."

To "prevent recurrence," Prudential Life Insurance is considering extending the period during which it will suspend new contracts, which was set to end on May 9, by about six months, the report said, in an English language translation.

The report also said "Prudential Holdings Japan, the parent company, will make an announcement soon" as external experts investigate the extent of the damage.

Prudential Financial could not immediately be reached.

Shares of Prudential Financial were down 6% on Tuesday.

The Asahi Shimbun newspaper has reported that 107 current and former Prudential Life Insurance employees "obtained money through fraudulent means or failed to repay funds they had borrowed from roughly 500 customers," amounting to an estimated 3.14 billion yen ($20 million) between 1991 and 2025, citing the company's internal investigation released on Jan. 16.

A separate investigation found that "69 employees violated internal rules by taking kickbacks for referring customers to outside investment firms," The Asahi Shimbun reported.

In early February, Prudential announced a voluntary 90-day suspension of new sales activity to support Prudential of Japan's implementation of comprehensive operational, organizational, and governance changes to address the previously disclosed incidents of misconduct by employees.

On April 9, the newspaper said that Japan's Financial Services Agency was expanding its investigation and conducting an on-site inspection of the parent company of Prudential Life Insurance Co. and examining whether "an excessive performance-driven culture that fostered misconduct and lax oversight of sales employees" contributed to what happened.

Prudential shares had traded as low as $95.74 on Tuesday afternoon, down 6.7% and on pace for its largest percentage decrease since April 4, 2025, when shares fell 7.9%, according to Dow Jones Market Data.

Shares closed down 6% at $96.45 on Tuesday. Prudential's stock is down 14.6% this year and down 3.8% over the past 12 months.

Write to Janet H. Cho at janet.cho@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Boeing Earnings Are Coming. Can They Avoid the Aerospace 'Bloodbath?' — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Al Root

Boeing reports quarterly results on Wednesday. Progress, not perfection, is the goal.

For the quarter, Wall Street projects a per share loss of 68 cents from sales of $21.3 billion, according to FactSet. A year ago, Boeing reported a per share loss of 49 cents from sales of $19.5 billion.

Sales are up on higher jet deliveries. Boeing delivered 143 commercial planes in the first three months of the year, up from 130 a year ago.

Growth is good. Still, "it's been a slow start to the year for Airbus and Boeing, with both OEMs being impacted by manufacturing issues (A320 fuselage panels, 737 wiring)," wrote Vertical Research Partners analyst Rob Stallard in a preview report. The underlying supply chain, however, should be OK with steady progress allowing the plane makers to boost 2026 production.

Boeing is expected to deliver about 660 planes in 2026, up from 600 in 2025.

Boeing is a turnaround story, which might insulate it from other aerospace concerns, such as the problem of high oil prices, which can impact demand for air travel.

Tuesday wasn't pretty for aerospace and defense companies. Shares of GE Aerospace, RTX, and Northrop Grumman fell about 6%, 4%, and 7%, respectively, on Tuesday. All three reported better-than-expected first quarter numbers.

It was a "bloodbath," said Stallard. "While it may seem pretty obvious, we think investors have been reluctant to acknowledge that the War in the Middle East is more negative for the aerospace sector than understood. We're not talking a COVID style Aero-mageddon, but in our estimate a continuation of Middle Eastern flight schedule reductions could take about 3% off global [traffic] growth this year."

The war could be reflected in Boeing's commentary about deliveries, which could be back-end loaded.

Boeing still has some internal issues that can move shares, such as approvals of new jet versions.

"We view certification of the -7 [version] and especially the -10 [version of the] MAX variant as very important for Boeing's margin profile in 2027," wrote RBC analyst Ken Herbert in a preview report. "We believe the -10 carries a strong pricing profile and will likely be critical for margins inflecting positive in 2027."

Updates on plane certifications by aviation authorities are another thing for investors to watch for.

Coming into the week, shares were down roughly 10% since the first quarter earnings report and down about 2% since the fighting started in Iran.

Write to Al Root at allen.root@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Society Pass Received Notice of Noncompliance
data.description:
On April 16, 2026, the company received a Nasdaq notice that it is not in compliance with Listing Rule 5250(c)(1) for failing to timely file its Form 10-K for the year ended December 31, 2025. The notice has no immediate effect; the company has 60 days to submit a plan (by June 15, 2026) and, if accepted, may have until October 12, 2026 to regain compliance.

Original SEC Filing: SOCIETY PASS INCORPORATED. [ SOPA ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: NETSTREIT launches $400M at-the-market equity program, terminates prior $300M ATM
data.description:
NETSTREIT launched a new at-the-market equity program to sell up to $400 million of common stock and terminated its prior ATM agreement.

Key Highlights:

  • Entered a sales agreement with multiple agents to offer up to $400 million of common stock through an at-the-market program.
  • Agents’ commissions capped at 1.5%; forward-sale related commissions also capped at 1.5% of gross sales price.
  • Proceeds intended to be contributed to the operating partnership for acquisitions, development, investments, working capital and debt repayment.
  • Terminated prior ATM; had sold roughly $256.1 million under the prior agreement and has 12,777,902 forward-settlement shares maturing Sept 2026–Mar 2027.

Original SEC Filing: NETSTREIT Corp. [ NTST ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Journey Medical expands Emrosi payer access to 85% of US commercial lives
data.description:
Journey Medical announced Emrosi payer access now reaches ~85% of U.S. commercial lives following a third major GPO contract.

Key Highlights:

  • Contract with a third major GPO expands Emrosi access from ~100M to over 150M commercial lives as of April 1, 2026.
  • Approximately 85% of all U.S. commercial lives now have access to Emrosi for inflammatory lesions of rosacea in adults.
  • Expanded payer access is expected to facilitate growth in prescription demand and support continued launch execution.
  • Company aims to convert access into formulary coverage, reducing reliance on co-pay bridging and improving brand profitability.

Original SEC Filing: Journey Medical Corp [ DERM ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Independent Bank Presents 2025 Results, $68.5M Net Income and $1.04 Dividend at 2026 Annual Meeting
data.description:
Independent Bank reported 2025 net income of $68.5 million and announced a $1.04 per share cash dividend at its 2026 annual meeting presentation.

Key Highlights:

  • Reported 2025 net income of $68.5M and diluted EPS of $3.27, reflecting continued profitability.
  • Paid $1.04 per share in cash dividends for 2025, an 8% increase year-over-year.
  • Loan growth of $237.5M (5.9%); commercial loans grew $276.2M (14.3%); loan-to-deposit ratio 89.8%.
  • Tangible book value per share rose $2.71 (13.4%) to $23.04; returned 31.5% of 2025 earnings via dividends.
  • Asset quality remained strong with reserves/loans at 1.48% and NPAs/assets of 0.44% for 2025.

Original SEC Filing: INDEPENDENT BANK CORP /MI/ [ IBCP ] - 8-K - Apr. 21, 2026

data.title: Assertio to Face Tender Offer by Garda at $18.00/Share; Total Cash Consideration $125.1M
data.description:
Assertio announced Garda will commence a tender offer to acquire all outstanding shares at $18.00 per share.

Key Highlights:

  • Garda intends to start a tender offer for all Assertio shares on April 29, 2026.
  • Acquisition price is $18.00 per share in cash, implying total cash consideration of $125.1 million.
  • Transaction includes a contingent value right in addition to the cash consideration.
  • Agreement contains a 20-day window-shop permitting Assertio to solicit superior bids; reduced breakup fee if terminated for a superior offer.

data.title: Interactive Brokers' quarterly profit rises on robust trading activity
data.description:
Trading platform Interactive Brokers reported a 25% jump in first-quarter profit on Tuesday, helped by higher customer trading volumes.

The quarter was marked by market volatility, driven by concerns over AI disrupting business models and an escalating conflict in the Middle East that rattled oil markets.

Volatility is often a boon for trading desks, as investors rejig portfolios to hedge risks.

Here are some details:

  • Commission revenue surged 19% to $613 million compared to the previous year, driven by a strong jump in customer stock, futures and options trading volumes in the three months ended March 31.

  • Net interest income rose 17% to $904 million for the quarter, owing to higher average customer margin loans and credit balances.

  • The Connecticut-based company allows customers to buy and sell stocks, options, futures, cryptocurrencies, bonds, mutual funds, ETFs and precious metals.

  • Daily average revenue trades, or DARTs, which represent the average trades per day generating commissions or fees, rose 24% in the quarter to 4.37 million trades.

  • The company reported net income available for common stockholders of $267 million or 59 cents per share for the first quarter, compared with $213 million or 48 cents per share in the previous year.

data.title: Sunwest Bank Has Entered into a Definitive Agreement to Acquire the Former MidWestOne and Bank of Denver Branches from Nicolet National Bank
data.description:
SANDY, Utah and DENVER, April 21, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Sunwest Bank, the Bank for Entrepreneurs by Entrepreneurs, has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire the former MidWestOne and Bank of Denver branches from Nicolet National Bank.

This acquisition furthers Sunwest's expansion across the Rocky Mountain region by significantly increasing its presence in Colorado. This expansion allows Sunwest to further invest in the Denver market by increasing the bank's client base, branch presence, and team of experienced banking professionals. Sunwest initially launched in the Colorado market in 2025 and since has hired Kari Coover as Colorado Market President, Donika McKelvie as National Director of Healthcare Banking, and Joe Seroke as Managing Director of Commercial Real Estate nationwide.

"The acquisition of the former Bank of Denver and MidWestOne branches is a significant milestone for our growth in Colorado," said Carson Lappetito, President of Sunwest Bank. "We are investing heavily in Denver and look forward to delivering the exceptional customer experience we are known for to entrepreneurs and clients in Denver and throughout the state of Colorado."

Sunwest is expanding its presence through the acquisition of the former Bank of Denver and MidWestOne banking offices, bringing its established strength, security, and customer experience to a broader client base. Founded in 1969, the bank has built a reputation for combining advanced technology with high-touch customer service. This expansion adds greater product depth and diversity, deep market expertise, and direct access to decision-makers, enhancing the overall client experience and supporting business growth.

"We are building something special here a team of top talent committed to delivering strategic guidance, creative solutions, and exceptional service. I look forward to welcoming new businesses, strengthening existing relationships, and growing market share across Colorado by helping our clients turn opportunity into lasting success," said Kari Coover, Sunwest Bank's Colorado Market Area President.

This expansion further strengthens their commitment to the market and allows Sunwest to offer a broader branch network to their existing customers and enhanced banking and innovative solutions to their newly acquired clients.

According to Robert Faver, Sunwest Bank's Chief Banking Officer, "Our continued expansion across the Mountain West creates a strong foundation for future growth. We have a strong leadership team in Colorado that is deeply connected to the market and has a long history of providing high-touch relationship-focused banking."

About Sunwest Bank

Founded in 1969, Sunwest Bank is a privately held commercial bank with over $4.0 billion in assets. With a growing presence throughout the United States, Sunwest is headquartered in Sandy, Utah, with offices across California, Arizona, Idaho, Colorado, Utah, and Florida. The bank partners with businesses, individuals, and entrepreneurs nationwide to deliver leading banking services including technology forward treasury management, commercial and real estate lending products, and corporate financial solutions.

With a strong capital position and a commitment to innovation, Sunwest Bank continues to challenge traditional banking models through forward-thinking initiatives designed to support its client's growth and long-term success. Sunwest Bank operates with a Fortress Balance Sheet, long-term outlook, and has an impeccable track record of supporting their clients through all economic cycles.

Sunwest Bank provides:

  • Consistency and reliability -- unwavering stability through economic cycles, mergers, and system transitions.
  • Direct access to decision-makers -- no call centers, just responsive, relationship-driven service all the way to the top of the organization.
  • Financial strength and security -- through prudent credit and balance sheet management, Sunwest's Fortress Balance Sheet and culture allow them to navigate all economic cycles.
  • A conservative, forward-looking approach -- ensuring that its strength continues to translate into opportunity for the businesses they serve.

Sunwest Bank remains strong, stable, and focused on its clients. Their entrepreneurial culture allows them to maintain the flexibility and responsiveness that other institutions often lose as they grow. Sunwest believes strength begins with discipline, and their financial foundation reflects that philosophy.

For Entrepreneurs by Entrepreneurs, Sunwest Bank is a Member FDIC and Equal Housing Lender. More information can be found at www.sunwestbank.com.

To stay updated on Sunwest Bank's continued Colorado expansion and the progress of this acquisition, please visit: www.sunwestbank.com/bank-of-denver-acquisition.

About Nicolet Bankshares, Inc.

Nicolet Bankshares, Inc. is a bank holding company of Nicolet National Bank, a growing, full-service, community bank providing services ranging from commercial, agricultural and consumer banking to wealth management and retirement plan services. Founded in Green Bay in 2000, Nicolet National Bank operates branches primarily in Wisconsin, Michigan, Minnesota, and Iowa. More information can be found at www.nicoletbank.com.

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/sunwest-bank-has-entered-into-a-definitive-agreement-to-acquire-the-former-midwestone-and-bank-of-denver-branches-from-nicolet-national-bank-302749374.html

SOURCE Sunwest Bank

data.title: Otis Raises Quarterly Dividend by 5%
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

Elevator and escalator company Otis Worldwide said its board approved a 5% increase of its quarterly dividend, to 44 cents per share from 42 cents.

The new dividend will be payable June 12 to shareholders of record at the close of business May 15.

Otis previously raised its dividend in April of last year, to 42 cents per share from 39 cents.

The new dividend, $1.76 on an annual basis, represents a yield of 2.2%, based on Tuesday's closing share price of $78.87.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: GM delays next-gen EV truck program, Crain's Detroit reports
data.description: General Motors is indefinitely delaying its next-generation full-size electric truck program, including the GMC Sierra and Chevrolet Silverado, that was set to start in 2028, Crain’s Detroit Business reported on Tuesday, citing sources.

data.title: Prudential Financial's Japan unit probed over alleged misconduct cases, Nikkei reports
data.description:
A second Japanese unit of American insurer Prudential Financial is suspected of seeing several instances of misconduct by former employees, Nikkei Asia reported on Wednesday.

Dozens of cases surfaced during an investigation where former employees of the second Japanese unit, Gibraltar Life Insurance, were suspected of mishandling customer funds, Nikkei added, citing unnamed sources.

data.title: Toll Brothers to Acquire Home Builder Buffington in Arkansas
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Toll Brothers plans to acquire the assets of Buffington Homes of Arkansas, a privately held homebuilder based in Arkansas.

The homebuilder said Tuesday the deal aims to bolster its footprint with a presence in the growing Fayetteville and Bentonville market in northwest Arkansas. Buffington Homes is the largest luxury home builder in the region with nine active selling or coming-soon communities, Toll Brothers said.

As part of the deal, Buffington Homes' senior leadership and employees will join Toll Brothers. Clay Carlton and Mike Lamberth, Buffington Homes' founding partners and co-owners, will focus on land acquisition and development. Mike Buffington, co-owner and the brother of late founder Thomas Buffington, is stepping away from the business to focus on his family, Toll Brothers said.

The companies didn't disclose the financial terms of the deal. Toll Brothers expects the transaction to close in its fiscal third quarter.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com
